articles.title: Hurricane Kiko’s path shifts, sparing Hawaii from strong winds and heavy rain
articles.content: Maximum sustained winds were 130 mph (215 kph), the weather service said. At a Category 4 hurricane Saturday, Kiko was expected to gradually weaken over the next several days. On Friday, acting Hawaii Gov. Sylvia Luke issued an emergency proclamation declaring a state of emergency to prepare for possible inclement weather from Kiko. “The threat for any wind is diminishing,” Joseph Clark, a meteorologist with the weather service in Honolulu, said Saturday. “With the storm going north, it tends to actually make the winds lighter than normal over the islands
articles.summary:
📰 제목: 허리케인 키코, 하와이 강타 피하고 북상

내용: 시속 215km의 강풍을 동반한 허리케인 키코가 하와이를 비켜 북상할 전망이다. 하와이 주지사가 긴급상태를 선포했으나, 조셉 클라크 기상청 대변인에 따르면 키코가 북상하며 하와이에 부는 바람은 오히려 평소보다 약할 것이라고 한다. 키코는 향후 몇 일간 세력이 약화될 것으로 예상된다.
